answer: First aid basics: ensure scene safety, call emergency services if needed, stop bleeding, maintain airway and breathing, and treat for shock. For specific injuries ask me for step-by-step guidance.

If someone has chest pain with sweating, nausea or breathlessness, call 108/112 immediately. Keep them calm, help them sit upright and loosen tight clothing. If trained, start CPR if they lose consciousness.
For suspected poisoning, call local poison control immediately and do not induce vomiting unless instructed. If the person is unconscious, place them in recovery position and call emergency services.
Apply direct pressure using a clean cloth or bandage. Elevate the injured area if possible. If bleeding is severe or does not stop within a few minutes, call emergency services immediately.
Cool the burn under running water for 10-20 minutes. Do not apply ice, toothpaste, or butter. Cover with a clean cloth and seek medical help for large or deep burns.
Keep the injured part still and avoid moving it. Use a splint if trained and seek medical care immediately.
Keep the person calm and immobilize the affected limb. Do not cut the wound or suck the venom. Seek emergency medical care immediately.
Wash the wound thoroughly with soap and water for at least 15 minutes. Apply a clean dressing and seek medical attention for rabies and tetanus evaluation.
Lay the person flat and raise their legs slightly. Check breathing and loosen tight clothing. If they do not wake up quickly, call emergency services.
Encourage the person to cough. If they cannot breathe or speak, perform abdominal thrusts if trained and call emergency services immediately.
Remember FAST: Face drooping, Arm weakness, Speech difficulty, Time to call emergency services. Immediate treatment is critical.
Move dangerous objects away and protect the person's head. Do not put anything in their mouth. Call emergency services if the seizure lasts more than 5 minutes.
Turn off the power source before touching the victim. Check breathing and pulse and begin CPR if necessary. Call emergency services immediately.
Remove the person from the water if safe to do so. Check breathing and begin CPR if they are not breathing. Call emergency services immediately.
Sit upright and lean slightly forward. Pinch the soft part of the nose for 10-15 minutes. Seek medical care if bleeding continues.
Drink plenty of fluids and rest. Seek medical attention if the fever is very high, lasts several days, or is accompanied by difficulty breathing or confusion.
Drink water or oral rehydration solution slowly. Seek medical care if there is confusion, severe weakness, or inability to drink fluids.
For mild reactions, avoid the allergen and monitor symptoms. If there is swelling of the face, difficulty breathing, or collapse, call emergency services immediately.
Move the person to a cool place, remove excess clothing, and cool them with wet cloths. Seek emergency care if they become confused or unconscious.
Move the person to a warm place and remove wet clothing. Warm them gradually and seek medical attention if severe.
Help the person sit upright and use their prescribed inhaler. Call emergency services if breathing does not improve.
If the person is conscious, give a sugary drink or glucose tablets. If unconscious, seek emergency medical help immediately.
Do not rub the eye. Rinse with clean water if appropriate and seek medical care for persistent pain or vision problems.
Wash the wound with clean water, apply an antiseptic, and cover with a sterile dressing.
Follow the RICE method: Rest, Ice, Compression, and Elevation.
